coordinate_frame.name: HRA Visible Human Female common frame
coordinate_frame.screen_laterality: patient left (+X) appears on image right in a conventional anterior view

acetabulum.derivation: Center is the mean surface point within 0.1 mm of ilium, ischium, and pubis. Rim radii are the 95th-percentile absolute XY offsets of local pairwise bone seams.

iliac_crest.derivation: Eleven-bin superior envelope of the official compact ilium surface.

acetabulum.derivation: Center is the mean surface point within 0.1 mm of ilium, ischium, and pubis. Rim radii are the 95th-percentile absolute XY offsets of local pairwise bone seams.

iliac_crest.derivation: Eleven-bin superior envelope of the official compact ilium surface.

fit.kind: frontal contour plates with true holes and sampled depth profiles

integration_recipe.occlusion_note: Render pelvic bone behind bowel/bladder but keep acetabular rims and iliac crests visible. Avoid torus-only obturator rings: the fitted outer plates and actual holes carry the recognizable anatomy.
priority_order: Create each hip as a THREE.Shape from outer_contour_xy_m.
Add obturator_foramen as a THREE.Path hole before ExtrudeGeometry.
Extrude shallowly and bend/offset Z using depth_profile_by_superior_fraction; do not extrude through the full atlas depth.
Add acetabular rims as elliptical torus or beveled rings at the fitted centers and radii.
Create sacrum and coccyx as separate tapered plates so their midline silhouette remains visible.
Place patient-left geometry at +X; do not mirror the already bilateral coefficient set.
integration_recipe.recommended_visual_depth: Use approximately 12-18 mm plate depth for the hip surfaces and 16-24 mm for the sacrum, then apply the sampled median-Z profile. Full source AABB depth is anatomical curvature, not slab thickness.

source_file.selection_rule: compact ilium/ischium/pubis for both sides plus sacrum and coccyx; nested spongy meshes excluded

validation.image_legend: white overlap; red official source only; blue procedural reconstruction only
